title: Exchange Rate Limit 429 Error Schema
description: Structured logging schema for rate limit (429) errors from exchange APIs. Use this to ensure your logs are consistent and queryable.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the 429 error occurred

endpoint.description: The API endpoint that was rate limited

http_status.description: HTTP status code (should always be 429 for this schema)

retry_after_header.description: Value of the Retry-After header from the exchange response (seconds or HTTP-date)

request_weight.description: Weight/cost of this single request (if applicable)

cumulative_weight_used.description: Total weight used in the current rate limit window

window_duration_seconds.description: Duration of the rate limit window (e.g., 60 for 1-minute window)

retry_count.description: Which retry attempt this is (1 = first 429, 2 = first retry, etc.)

max_retries.description: Maximum retries configured for this endpoint

base_wait_seconds.description: Base wait time for backoff calculation

wait_duration_ms.description: How long we actually waited before this retry (in milliseconds)

jitter_applied_ms.description: Random jitter added to backoff (in milliseconds)

reason_for_429.description: Inferred reason from the response body or headers

request_id.description: Exchange-provided request ID for tracing (if available)

user_agent.description: User-Agent header (some exchanges rate-limit per user agent)

ip_address.description: IP address making the request (useful for detecting shared infrastructure issues)

strategy_name.description: Name of the trading strategy that triggered this request

total_requests_in_window.description: Total requests made in this window (before 429)

consecutive_429_count.description: How many 429s in a row (0 if this is the first)

severity.description: Severity level (info = expected, warning = unusual, error = potential ban)

tags.description: Additional tags for filtering and alerting
